revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iwanari Tomomichi]][[fa:ایواناری تومومیچی]][[ff:Iwanari Tomomichi]][[fr:Iwanari Tomomichi]][[ja:石成友通]]
{{Infobox person|image=File:Iwanari Tomomichi.jpg|birth_date=1519|death_date={{death date|1573|8|29}}; {{death date|1578|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|native_name=石成友通}}

{{More citations needed|date=February 2022}}
[[File:Iwanari Tomomichi.jpg|thumb|Portrait of Iwanari Tomomichi from {{ill|Utagawa Yoshiiku|en|Utagawa Yoshiiku|qid=Q2482193|lt=Utagawa Yoshiiku|1=Utagawa Yoshiiku}}'s ''Heroes of the Taiheiki'']]
{{family name hatnote|Iwanari|lang=Japanese}}

{{nihongo|'''Iwanari Tomomichi'''|岩成 友通||1519 – August 29, 1578}} was a Japanese samurai of the 16th century. Also known as Ishinari Tomomichi (石成友通), he was a senior retainer of the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|qid=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E5%A5%BD%E4%B8%89%E4%BA%BA%E8%A1%86-139812|title=朝日日本歴史人物事典「岩成友通」の解説|publisher=Kotobank|access-date=15 December 2021}}</ref> He was one of the three great samurai of the Miyoshi clan called ''Miyoshi Sanninshu'' along with Miyoshi Nagayuki and Miyoshi Masayasu.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E5%A5%BD%E4%B8%89%E4%BA%BA%E8%A1%86-139812|title=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)「三好三人衆」の解説|publisher=Kotobank|access-date=15 December 2021}}</ref> Leading an uprising against the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} toward the end of his life, Iwanari was killed in battle by {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|en|Hosokawa Fujitaka|qid=Q1207509|lt=Hosokawa Fujitaka|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka}}.
